He did not experience nausea, vomiting, diarrhea, or changes in bowel movements but did have flatus. The patient was concerned about peritonitis due to the cloudy appearance of his PD fluid. His blood pressure is chronically low, with a baseline in the 80s. Initial vital signs in the ED were BP 68/27, HR 106, RR 16, SpO2 97%, and temperature 98.4°F. Upon arrival, he was pleasant and in no acute distress (NAD), complaining only of abdominal pain.
